rulings.Case Title: KURT GRIMES VS. RUSHMORE LOAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 12, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action in good faith in light of all arguments raised in the moving papers. Plaintiff's oversized brief in support of opposition is stricken. (This is the second time Plaintiff submits an oversized brief without leave of Court. The Court considered the original oversized brief; since Plaintiff chose to file an oversized brief again, the Court has no choice but to enforce the rules this time). Plaintiff shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. The Court notes that the oversized opposition failed to address or even recognize multiple arguments and authorities raised in the moving papers. Plaintiff must familiarize themselves with those arguments and authorities and address them in drafting the amended complaint. All new allegations in the amended complaint must be in bold font. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PAUL H. NATHAN JR VS. JUAN MORALES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-612254, PES-23-306908 WITH CASE CUD-23-673698
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 12, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT JUAN ROSALES' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASES CGC-24-612254, PES-23-306908 WITH CASE CUD-23-673698 is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to consolidate the civil action and unlawful detainer action with the probate proceeding is denied. Motion to consolidate the unlawful detainer action and the civil action is granted. For the duration of the pendency of this action, defendant to post with the Court $2,000/month. Defendant shall post $1,333 on March 15, 2024 and $2,000 on the first business day of each month thereafter. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
